objects.description: The source platform of the key event's campaign. Don't depend on this field returning `Manual` for traffic that uses UTMs; this field will update from returning `Manual` to returning `(not set)` for an upcoming feature launch.
objects.name: sourcePlatform

objects.description: The numeric data stream identifier for your app or website.
objects.name: streamId

objects.description: The data stream name for your app or website.
objects.name: streamName

objects.description: The numeric identifier of a data filter in testing state. You use data filters to include or exclude event data from your reports based on event-parameter values. To learn more, see [Data filters](https://support.google.com/analytics/answer/10108813).
objects.name: testDataFilterId

objects.description: The name of data filters in testing state. You use data filters to include or exclude event data from your reports based on event-parameter values. To learn more, see [Data filters](https://support.google.com/analytics/answer/10108813).
objects.name: testDataFilterName

objects.description: The ID of the ecommerce transaction.
objects.name: transactionId

objects.description: The page path (web) or screen class (app) on which the event was logged.
objects.name: unifiedPagePathScreen

objects.description: The page path and query string (web) or screen class (app) on which the event was logged.
objects.name: unifiedPageScreen

objects.description: The page title (web) or screen class (app) on which the event was logged.
objects.name: unifiedScreenClass

objects.description: The page title (web) or screen name (app) on which the event was logged.
objects.name: unifiedScreenName

objects.description: User age brackets.
objects.name: userAgeBracket

objects.description: User gender.
objects.name: userGender

objects.description: The source of the video (for example, `youtube`). Automatically populated for embedded videos if Enhanced Measurement is enabled. Populated by the event parameter `video_provider`.
objects.name: videoProvider

objects.description: The title of the video. Automatically populated for embedded videos if Enhanced Measurement is enabled. Populated by the event parameter `video_title`.
objects.name: videoTitle

objects.description: The URL of the video. Automatically populated for embedded videos if Enhanced Measurement is enabled. Populated by the event parameter `video_url`.
objects.name: videoUrl

objects.description: The name of a virtual currency with which the user is interacting. Such as spending or purchasing gems in a game. Populated by the `virtual_currency_name` event parameter.
objects.name: virtualCurrencyName

objects.description: Returns `true` if the content is visible. Automatically populated for embedded videos if Enhanced Measurement is enabled. Populated by the event parameter `visible`.
objects.name: visible

objects.description: The week of the event, a two-digit number from 01 to 53. Each week starts on Sunday. January 1st is always in week 01. The first and last week of the year have fewer than 7 days in most years. Weeks other than the first and the last week of the year always have 7 days. For years where January 1st is a Sunday, the first week of that year and the last week of the prior year have 7 days.
objects.name: week

objects.description: The four-digit year of the event. For example, 2020 or 2024.
objects.name: year

objects.description: The combined values of year and month. Example values include 202212 or 202301.
objects.name: yearMonth

objects.description: The combined values of year and week. Example values include 202253 or 202301.
objects.name: yearWeek

objects.description: The number of distinct active users on your site or app within a 1 day period. The 1 day period includes the last day in the report's date range. Note: this is the same as Active Users.
objects.name: active1DayUsers

objects.description: The number of distinct active users on your site or app within a 28 day period. The 28 day period includes the last day in the report's date range.
objects.name: active28DayUsers

objects.description: The number of distinct active users on your site or app within a 7 day period. The 7 day period includes the last day in the report's date range.
objects.name: active7DayUsers

objects.description: The number of distinct users who visited your site or app.
objects.name: activeUsers

objects.description: The time that an ad unit was exposed to a user, in milliseconds.
objects.name: adUnitExposure

objects.description: The number of times users added items to their shopping carts.
objects.name: addToCarts

objects.description: Total number of times users have clicked on an ad to reach the property. Includes clicks from linked integrations like linked Search Ads 360 advertisers. Also includes uploaded clicks from data import.
objects.name: advertiserAdClicks

objects.description: The total amount you paid for your ads. Includes costs from linked integrations like linked Google Ads accounts. Also includes uploaded cost from data import; to learn more, see [Import cost data](https://support.google.com/analytics/answer/10071305).
objects.name: advertiserAdCost

objects.description: Ads cost per click is ad cost divided by ad clicks and is often abbreviated CPC.
objects.name: advertiserAdCostPerClick

objects.description: Cost per key event is ad cost divided by key events.
objects.name: advertiserAdCostPerKeyEvent

objects.description: The total number of impressions. Includes impressions from linked integrations like linked Display & Video 360 advertisers. Also includes uploaded impressions from data import.
objects.name: advertiserAdImpressions

objects.description: The average purchase revenue in the transaction group of events.
objects.name: averagePurchaseRevenue

objects.description: Average revenue per paying user (ARPPU) is the total purchase revenue per active user that logged a purchase event. The summary metric is for the time period selected.
objects.name: averagePurchaseRevenuePerPayingUser

objects.description: The average purchase revenue per active user is the total purchase revenue per active user that logged any event. The summary metric is for the time period selected.
objects.name: averagePurchaseRevenuePerUser

objects.description: Average revenue per active user (ARPU). The summary metric is for the time period selected. ARPU uses Total Revenue and includes AdMob estimated earnings.
objects.name: averageRevenuePerUser

objects.description: The average duration (in seconds) of users` sessions.
objects.name: averageSessionDuration

objects.description: The percentage of sessions that were not engaged ((Sessions Minus Engaged sessions) divided by Sessions). This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.2761 means 27.61% of sessions were bounces.
objects.name: bounceRate

objects.description: The number of users who added a product(s) to their cart divided by the number of users who viewed the same product(s). This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.1132 means 11.32% of users who viewed a product also added the same product to their cart.
objects.name: cartToViewRate

objects.description: The number of times users started the checkout process. This metric counts the occurrence of the `begin_checkout` event.
objects.name: checkouts

objects.description: The number of users in the cohort who are active in the time window corresponding to the cohort nth day/week/month. For example in the row where cohortNthWeek = 0001, this metric is the number of users (in the cohort) who are active in week 1.
objects.name: cohortActiveUsers

objects.description: The total number of users in the cohort. This metric is the same value in every row of the report for each cohort. Because cohorts are defined by a shared acquisition date, cohortTotalUsers is the same as cohortActiveUsers for the cohort's selection date range. For report rows later than the cohort's selection range, it is typical for cohortActiveUsers to be smaller than cohortTotalUsers. This difference represents users from the cohort that were not active for the later date. cohortTotalUsers is commonly used in the metric expression cohortActiveUsers/cohortTotalUsers to compute a user retention fraction for the cohort. The relationship between activeUsers and totalUsers is not equivalent to the relationship between cohortActiveUsers and cohortTotalUsers.
objects.name: cohortTotalUsers

objects.description: The number of users that logged a crash in this row of the report. For example if the report is time series by date, this metrics reports total users with at least one crash on this date. Crashes are events with the name "app_exception".
objects.name: crashAffectedUsers

objects.description: The number of users without crash events (in this row of the report) divided by the total number of users. This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.9243 means 92.43% of users were crash-free.
objects.name: crashFreeUsersRate

objects.description: The rolling percent of 30-day active users who are also 1-day active users. This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.113 means 11.3% of 30-day active users were also 1-day active users.
objects.name: dauPerMau

objects.description: The rolling percent of 7-day active users who are also 1-day active users. This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.082 means 8.2% of 7-day active users were also 1-day active users.
objects.name: dauPerWau

objects.description: The number of times users completed a purchase. This metric counts `purchase` events; this metric does not count `in_app_purchase` and subscription events.
objects.name: ecommercePurchases

objects.description: The number of sessions that lasted longer than 10 seconds, or had a key event, or had 2 or more screen views.
objects.name: engagedSessions

objects.description: The percentage of engaged sessions (Engaged sessions divided by Sessions). This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.7239 means 72.39% of sessions were engaged sessions.
objects.name: engagementRate

objects.description: The count of events.
objects.name: eventCount

objects.description: The average number of events per user (Event count divided by Active users).
objects.name: eventCountPerUser

objects.description: The sum of the event parameter named `value`.
objects.name: eventValue

objects.description: The average number of events per session (Event count divided by Sessions).
objects.name: eventsPerSession

objects.description: The percentage of active users who made their first purchase. This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.092 means 9.2% of active users were first time purchasers.
objects.name: firstTimePurchaserRate

objects.description: The number of users that completed their first purchase event.
objects.name: firstTimePurchasers

objects.description: The average number of first time purchasers per new user.
objects.name: firstTimePurchasersPerNewUser

objects.description: The total revenue from items only. Gross item revenue is the product of its price and quantity. Item revenue excludes tax and shipping values; tax & shipping values are specified at the event and not item level. Gross item revenue does not include refunds.
objects.name: grossItemRevenue

objects.description: The sum of revenue from purchases made in your app or site. Gross purchase revenue sums the revenue for these events: `purchase`, `ecommerce_purchase`, `in_app_purchase`, `app_store_subscription_convert`, and `app_store_subscription_renew`. Purchase revenue is specified by the `value` parameter in tagging.
objects.name: grossPurchaseRevenue

objects.description: The monetary value of item discounts in eCommerce events. This metric is populated in tagging by the `discount` item parameter.
objects.name: itemDiscountAmount

objects.description: The number of times users clicked an item when it appeared in a list. This metric counts the occurrence of the `select_item` event.
objects.name: itemListClickEvents

objects.description: The number of users who selected a list(s) divided by the number of users who viewed the same list(s). This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.2145 means 21.45% of users who viewed a list also selected the same list.
objects.name: itemListClickThroughRate

objects.description: The number of times the item list was viewed. This metric counts the occurrence of the `view_item_list` event.
objects.name: itemListViewEvents

objects.description: The number of users who selected a promotion(s) divided by the number of users who viewed the same promotion(s). This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.1382 means 13.82% of users who viewed a promotion also selected the promotion.
objects.name: itemPromotionClickThroughRate

objects.description: Item refund amount is the total refunded transaction revenue from items only. Item refund amount is the product of price and quantity for the `refund` event.
objects.name: itemRefundAmount

objects.description: The total revenue from purchases minus refunded transaction revenue from items only. Item revenue is the product of its price and quantity. Item revenue excludes tax and shipping values; tax & shipping values are specified at the event and not item level.
objects.name: itemRevenue

objects.description: The number of times the item details were viewed. The metric counts the occurrence of the `view_item` event.
objects.name: itemViewEvents

objects.description: The number of units added to cart for a single item. This metric counts the quantity of items in `add_to_cart` events.
objects.name: itemsAddedToCart

objects.description: The number of units checked out for a single item. This metric counts the quantity of items in `begin_checkout` events.
objects.name: itemsCheckedOut

objects.description: The number of units clicked in list for a single item. This metric counts the quantity of items in `select_item` events.
objects.name: itemsClickedInList

objects.description: The number of units clicked in promotion for a single item. This metric counts the quantity of items in `select_promotion` events.
objects.name: itemsClickedInPromotion

objects.description: The number of units for a single item included in purchase events. This metric counts the quantity of items in purchase events.
objects.name: itemsPurchased

objects.description: The number of units viewed for a single item. This metric counts the quantity of items in `view_item` events.
objects.name: itemsViewed

objects.description: The number of units viewed in list for a single item. This metric counts the quantity of items in `view_item_list` events.
objects.name: itemsViewedInList

objects.description: The number of units viewed in promotion for a single item. This metric counts the quantity of items in `view_promotion` events.
objects.name: itemsViewedInPromotion

objects.description: The count of key events. Marking an event as a key event affects reports from time of creation. It doesn't change historic data. You can mark any event as key in Google Analytics, and some events (such as `first_open` or `purchase`) are marked as key events by default.
objects.name: keyEvents

objects.description: The count of a specific key event.
objects.name: keyEvents:purchase

objects.description: The number of users who interacted with your site or launched your app for the first time (event triggered: first_open or first_visit).
objects.name: newUsers

objects.description: The average ranking of your website URLs for the query reported from Search Console. For example, if your site's URL appears at position 3 for one query and position 7 for another query, the average position would be (3+7)/2, or 5. This metric requires an active Search Console link.
objects.name: organicGoogleSearchAveragePosition

objects.description: The organic Google Search click through rate reported from Search Console. Click through rate is clicks per impression. This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.0588 means about 5.88% of impressions resulted in a click. This metric requires an active Search Console link.
objects.name: organicGoogleSearchClickThroughRate

objects.description: The number of organic Google Search clicks reported from Search Console.  This metric requires an active Search Console link.
objects.name: organicGoogleSearchClicks

objects.description: The number of organic Google Search impressions reported from Search Console. This metric requires an active Search Console link.
objects.name: organicGoogleSearchImpressions

objects.description: The number of times an item promotion was clicked. This metric counts the occurrence of the `select_promotion` event.
objects.name: promotionClicks

objects.description: The number of times an item promotion was viewed. This metric counts the occurrence of the `view_promotion` event.
objects.name: promotionViews

objects.description: The number of ad_click events.
objects.name: publisherAdClicks

objects.description: The number of ad_impression events.
objects.name: publisherAdImpressions

objects.description: The sum of revenue from purchases minus refunded transaction revenue made in your app or site. Purchase revenue sums the revenue for these events: `purchase`, `ecommerce_purchase`, `in_app_purchase`, `app_store_subscription_convert`, and `app_store_subscription_renew`. Purchase revenue is specified by the `value` parameter in tagging.
objects.name: purchaseRevenue

objects.description: The number of users who purchased a product(s) divided by the number of users who viewed the same product(s). This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.128 means 12.8% of users that viewed a product(s) also purchased the same product(s).
objects.name: purchaseToViewRate

objects.description: The percentage of active users who made 1 or more purchase transactions. This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.412 means 41.2% of users were purchasers.
objects.name: purchaserRate

objects.description: The total refunded transaction revenues. Refund amount sums refunded revenue for the `refund` and `app_store_refund` events.
objects.name: refundAmount

objects.description: Return On Ad Spend (ROAS) is total revenue divided by advertiser ad cost.
objects.name: returnOnAdSpend

objects.description: The number of app screens or web pages your users viewed. Repeated views of a single page or screen are counted. (screen_view + page_view events).
objects.name: screenPageViews

objects.description: The number of app screens or web pages your users viewed per session. Repeated views of a single page or screen are counted. (screen_view + page_view events) / sessions.
objects.name: screenPageViewsPerSession

objects.description: The number of app screens or web pages your users viewed per active user. Repeated views of a single page or screen are counted. (screen_view + page_view events) / active users.
objects.name: screenPageViewsPerUser

objects.description: The number of unique users who scrolled down at least 90% of the page.
objects.name: scrolledUsers

objects.description: The percentage of sessions in which any key event was triggered.
objects.name: sessionKeyEventRate

objects.description: The percentage of sessions in which a specific key event was triggered.
objects.name: sessionKeyEventRate:purchase

objects.description: The number of sessions that began on your site or app (event triggered: session_start).
objects.name: sessions

objects.description: The average number of sessions per user (Sessions divided by Active Users).
objects.name: sessionsPerUser

objects.description: Shipping amount associated with a transaction. Populated by the `shipping` event parameter.
objects.name: shippingAmount

objects.description: Tax amount associated with a transaction. Populated by the `tax` event parameter.
objects.name: taxAmount

objects.description: The total advertising revenue from both Admob and third-party sources.
objects.name: totalAdRevenue

objects.description: The number of users that logged purchase events for the time period selected.
objects.name: totalPurchasers

objects.description: The sum of revenue from purchases, subscriptions, and advertising (Purchase revenue plus Subscription revenue plus Ad revenue) minus refunded transaction revenue.
objects.name: totalRevenue

objects.description: The number of distinct users who have logged at least one event, regardless of whether the site or app was in use when that event was logged.
objects.name: totalUsers

objects.description: The count of transaction events with purchase revenue. Transaction events are in_app_purchase, ecommerce_purchase, purchase, app_store_subscription_renew, app_store_subscription_convert, and refund.
objects.name: transactions

objects.description: The average number of transactions per purchaser.
objects.name: transactionsPerPurchaser

objects.description: The total amount of time (in seconds) your website or app was in the foreground of users` devices.
objects.name: userEngagementDuration

objects.description: The percentage of users who triggered any key event.
objects.name: userKeyEventRate

objects.description: The percentage of users who triggered a specific key event.
objects.name: userKeyEventRate:purchase

objects.description: The rolling percent of 30-day active users who are also 7-day active users. This metric is returned as a fraction; for example, 0.234 means 23.4% of 30-day active users were also 7-day active users.
objects.name: wauPerMau
